name: Streamlit On Slurm
summary: Run policy-aware Streamlit apps from short Slurm compute allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for running Streamlit data apps on Slurm-backed HPC systems, including preflight checks, plan-only defaults, explicit port handling, optional tunnel guidance, and a tiny smoke-test app.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Used to run the Streamlit app inside a scheduled allocation.

tools.name: python3
tools.notes: Used to run the app and verify the Python environment.

tools.name: streamlit
tools.notes: The Streamlit CLI must be available in the selected Python environment.

tools.name: ssh
tools.notes: Used only when the site requires SSH port forwarding to reach compute-node web services.

inputs.name: app_path
inputs.description: Streamlit app entrypoint, such as streamlit_app.py.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Port where Streamlit should listen inside the Slurm allocation.

inputs.name: project_dir
inputs.description: Project directory used as the working directory for the Streamlit app.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local policy confirming whether user-started web apps and tunnels are allowed on compute nodes.

outputs.name: streamlit_log
outputs.description: Slurm output showing compute host, app path, selected port, and Streamlit startup output.

outputs.name: connection_notes
outputs.description: SSH tunnel or site portal notes for reaching the scheduled app.

examples.title: Plan or launch a Streamlit app on Slurm
Check Python, Streamlit, Slurm, and app prerequisites
Build an SSH tunnel command when required by the site
Review site policy before starting a web app

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
After site approval, run the preflight, submit the plan-only job, then set RUN_STREAMLIT_APP=1 in a short allocation and cancel it when finished.
